programme.title: Strange World S1 - "Alien Sounds"
programme.desc: Mysterious, alien sky sounds terrify Canadian residents, who struggle to locate their source.

programme.title: Strange World S1 - "Birds"
programme.desc: Host Alex heads to Arkansas, USA to investigate a Bird Apocalypse where the bodies of thousands of blackbirds rained down from the sky.

programme.title: Strange World S1 - "Killer Mice"
programme.desc: Alex heads to South Australia to investigate a terrifying rodent infestation.

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "Naples"
programme.desc: Naples is one of Europe’s oldest and most beautifully situated cities; a matrimonio of culture and nature, staring down the ravages of time. Few urban societies remain as closely connected to their past, which is proving both a blessing and a curse.

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "Seattle"
programme.desc: Innovation underscores this episode, set in one of the fastest growing cities in America. Ever on the cusp of discovering the “next big thing”, Seattle is where Amazon, Microsoft, Google are forging the future.

programme.title: How to Build a City S1 - "Berlin"
programme.desc: Berlin suffered from an image problem for decades. But the city at the epicentre of World War 2 is steadily gaining notoriety for all the right reasons. It’s the leading start up city in Europe; uber-cool and decidedly cosmopolitan.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Tornados"
programme.desc: George tracks a building storm that threatens that tough mid-western city, chases a hail storm, fights to get in the middle of a vicious dust-devil and the funky roadside Americana of the American Midwest

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Desert Monsoon"
programme.desc: Host/adventurer George Kourounis ups the danger factor by traveling with wild atheist shock-jock R.J. Evans to track massive lightning storms which can produce bolts of up to one hundred million volts.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "African Hellhole"
programme.desc: Host/adventurer George Kourounis explores the bubbling lake of molten lava and investigates the risk the dangerous volcano poses to both the people of city of Goma and the Mountain Gorillas who live on the nearby jungle slopes.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded S1 - "Megafloods"
programme.desc: Witness the devastating destructive power unleashed by planet earth’s most valuable resource – water.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded S1 - "Constructions"
programme.desc: Discover the worst bridge collapses and tunnel fires in history.

programme.title: Heavy Lifting S1 - "Under Construction"
programme.desc: Maxum swaps a grain handler, J.P. faces a tricky lift with a contractor, Brad and Nathan lift slabs in rain, and Dave visits a theme park.

programme.title: Heavy Lifting S1 - "Day or Night"
programme.desc: Dave handles a college AC unit and a historic ride, Brad and Nathan battle wind lifting trusses, and Dan and Francis work the night shift underground.

programme.title: Heavy Lifting S1 - "Ticket to Ride"
programme.desc: Brad and Nathan build a fire training unit, Dave works on amusement rides, and Dan and Francis lift a massive excavator from a construction pit.

programme.title: Secrets of the Factories S1 - "Secrets of Kellogg's Cereals"
programme.desc: In Kellogg’s first factory in Europe, built in the 1930s in Trafford Park, Manchester, we meet the people who flake the corn and puff the rice, the tasters who test, and the robots and hi-tech machines that help keep the boxes rolling out day and night.

programme.title: Made in Britain S1 - "Whitaker's Chocolates and Heck! Sausages"
programme.desc: Lord Prescott visits Whitaker’s chocolate factory to explore large-scale chocolate making, from factory lines to expert tasting and the company’s rich history. He then heads to HECK! Sausages in North Yorkshire, where he attempts to create his own custom recipe—the Hull banger.

programme.title: Space Greed S1 - "Endangered Aliens: Will Humans Trash Space?"
programme.sub-title: Endangered Aliens: Will Humans Trash Space?
programme.desc: Sci-Fi has always shocked audiences with images of powerful aliens invading and destroying Earth. What if WE are actually the invasive alien species?

programme.title: Space Greed S1 - "Space Cowboys: Who Will Wrangle Asteroids And Save The Earth?"
programme.sub-title: Space Cowboys: Who Will Wrangle Asteroids And Save The Earth?
programme.desc: The next time another huge space rock jeopardizes Earth, will private space companies and their advanced, expensive, and reusable spacecraft be enough to save the day?

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Conqeuring The Sky"
programme.desc: The evolution of mankind's desire to fly higher, move faster, and kill better all from within the cockpit.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Ultimate Annihilation"
programme.desc: Explore the destructive capability of machinery across all scales, the inventions designed to unleash maximum carnage, then and now.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "Targeting & Tracking, Navigation, Seagoing Drones"
programme.desc: We reveal an experimental sensor, monitoring and targeting system developed for troops by the UK's Defence Science and Technology Laboratory and Roke Manor Research. The iXblue Marins navigation black box guides navy ships.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "NATO Maritime Centre, No Shipping-No Shopping"
programme.sub-title: NATO Maritime Centre, No Shipping-No Shopping
programme.desc: We visit NATO's Centre for Maritime Research and Experimentation in La Spezia, Italy. We learn how the world's oceans are one of the last places that military technology can be hidden from prying eyes.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "Terrorist Threats, Sea Wasp Robot, Combat Medics"
programme.sub-title: Terrorist Threats, Sea Wasp Robot, Combat Medics
programme.desc: With an increasing terrorist threat to ports and shipping, the Swedish manufacturer SAAB has developed the Sea Wasp, an under-water robot for detecting explosive devices.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "Warrior Vehicles, British Special Forces"
programme.desc: We're in Scotland for Unmanned Warrior, the key international military exercise using unmanned underwater, surface and airborne vehicles. We visit the Italian textile manufacturer who's kitting from a revolutionary new form of wool.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Mastering Arms"
programme.desc: A weapon that has built Empires, toppled Kingdoms, and slaughtered millions, the story of the gun and how it has evolved throughout the centuries is long.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Advancing Destruction"
programme.desc: This episode tells the story of that journey showcasing both big and the small artillery and the impact they have had on warfare.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Triumphant On Land"
programme.desc: Highlighting some of the most influential movers ever to take to the battlefield including some of the most infamous tanks.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Ruling The Waves"
programme.desc: From the most deadly submarines ever seen to the infamous destroyers of the Second World War, this episode is the story of what it takes to rule the waves.

programme.title: Dictators' Hideouts S1 - "Mussolini's Underground Refuge"
programme.desc: 50 km north of Rome lies the greatest hideout in fascist Italy’s history: The Monte Soratte Bunker, with security features even a Bond villain could only dream of.

programme.title: Dictators' Hideouts S1 - "Hoxha's Bunker Madness"
programme.desc: Paranoid and isolated, Albanian dictator Enver Hoxha establishes his bunkerization project, which would result in the construction of more than 170,000 bunkers.
